---
title: "Module 1: Citation Management"
subtitle: "Organize your references effectively"
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
#### 🎯 Learning Objectives

After completing this module, you will be able to:

- Set up and use a citation management tool (Zotero or Paperpile)
- Organize and sync your reference library
- Generate citations and bibliographies in any citation style
- Understand what a Biosketch is and how to create one for grant applications
:::

---

## Overview

This module introduces citation management and two free tools: **Zotero** and **Paperpile**. It also covers the **Biosketch**, a document required for grant applications. Go through the summary texts on this page and review the presentations, recordings, guides, and other resources in the Resources Section to learn more.

> ✅ Complete the assignment at the end of this module and the associated knowledge check quiz on Canvas to earn credit toward your certificate!

---

## Why Citation Management Matters

Manually tracking hundreds of references is error-prone and time-consuming. Citation management software lets you:

- Capture references from databases, websites, and PDFs in one click
- Auto-generate citations and bibliographies in any style (APA, MLA, Chicago, etc.)
- Sync your library across devices and collaborate with team members
- Attach and annotate PDFs alongside your references

---

## Zotero vs. Paperpile

### Choosing the Right Tool

Both tools are free to start and cover similar core workflow. Review the presentation slides and recordings in the Resources Section to learn more.

| Feature | Zotero | Paperpile |
|---|---|---|
| Price | Free ([300 MB storage](https://www.zotero.org/storage)) | UCR Subscription using UCR email address |
| Best for | Word, LibreOffice, standalone use | Google Docs users |
| Browser extension | ✅ All browsers| ✅ Google Chrome|
| Collaboration | ✅ Group libraries | ✅ Shared folders |
| Mobile app | ✅ | ✅ |
| PDF annotation | ✅ Built-in | ✅ Built-in |

### [Zotero]((https://www.zotero.org/))

- Download at [zotero.org](https://www.zotero.org/) — freemium (subscription comes with more storage but the features are the same), open source
- Install the **Zotero Connector** browser extension to save references in one click
- Integrates with Microsoft Word, LibreOffice, and Google Docs
- Sync your library across devices with a free Zotero account

### [Paperpile](https://paperpile.com/)

- UCR Subscription using UCR email address
- Works entirely in the **Google Docs** environment — no separate app needed
- Install the browser extension and Google Docs add-on
- Best choice if your entire workflow lives in Google Drive

---

## Biosketches for Grant Applications

A **biosketch** is a short professional biography required by federal funders (NIH, NSF) as part of grant applications. It summarizes your education, positions, contributions to science, and research support.

- NIH and NSF each have slightly different biosketch formats
- The [UCR Library guide](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/c.php?g=962000&p=7732817) walks you through each section step by step
- Creating your biosketch early before grant deadlines saves significant time

---

## Resources

### Guides & Overviews

::: {.resource-list}
- [**Guide: Citation Management Overview**](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/referencemanagement) — UCR Library guide covering all citation tools available to students
  [Guide]{.resource-type}

- [**Guide: How to Cite Sources**](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/citing) — Style guides for APA, MLA, Chicago, and discipline-specific formats
  [Guide]{.resource-type}

- [**Guide: Creating Your Biosketch**](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/c.php?g=962000&p=7732817) — Step-by-step instructions for NIH and NSF biosketches
  [Guide]{.resource-type}
:::

### Zotero

::: {.resource-list}
- [**Slides: Citation Management with Zotero**](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Mxk8b7uPGbGAX7C_tOtYza4__-dZZ34J-IpDhBBaH_Q/edit?usp=sharing) — Presentation slides from UCR Library
  [Slides]{.resource-type .slides}

- [**Recording: Citation Series — Zotero & Paperpile**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=odYFJRKqEdM&list=PLaDWxNwvAu5bQrpeV7ORCiw6OY1a215Hm&index=1) — Video workshop recording
  [Video]{.resource-type .video}
:::

### Paperpile

::: {.resource-list}
- [**Slides: Paperpile — Citation Management for Google Docs**](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1fLMz5rMfYl_rVUcaZI3bCevpD6acj3Lh/edit?usp=sharing&rtpof=true&sd=true) — Presentation slides from UCR Library
  [Slides]{.resource-type .slides}
:::

---

## Assignment

::: {.callout-important}
### 📋 Assignment: Screenshots of Your Citation Management Tool

Set up a citation management tool (Zotero or Paperpile) and submit screenshots showing:

1. Your citation library with at least **3 references** imported
2. Evidence of browser extension installation OR a citation exported to a document

**Submission:** Upload your screenshots to the Canvas course platform.

*This assignment is required for the certificate.*
:::

---

## Get Help

- 🗓️ UCR Library offers **synchronous Zotero workshops** sometimes — check [library.ucr.edu](https://library.ucr.edu) for upcoming sessions
- 📧 **STEM Teaching Librarian:** Mary-Michelle Moore · [marymichelle.moore@ucr.edu](mailto:marymichelle.moore@ucr.edu)
- 📧 **LibSTEM Team:** [libstemteam@ucr.edu](mailto:libstemteam@ucr.edu)

---

```{=html}
<div class="next-module-banner">
  <a href="02-scholarly-communication.qmd">
    <div>
      <div class="next-label">Up Next · Module 2</div>
      <div class="next-title">Scholarly Communication</div>
    </div>
    <div class="next-arrow">→</div>
  </a>
</div>
```
